revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:稲毛神社]]



 


{{References|date=October 2013}} 
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| image                 = File:Kawasaki Inage-jinja Torii.jpg
| deity                 = [[Takemikazuchi]]
 | name=Inage Shrine
 | location={{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]], [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[Miyamotocho (Kawasaki)|Miyamotocho]] 7-7
 | coordinates={{Coord|35.530833333333334|N|139.70444444444445|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
 | deity=[[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami]]{{sfn|Edo Meisho Zue}}
 | type=Former [[Gōsha]]・[[Beppyo Shrine]]
 | festival={{ill|August 2|en|August 2|qid=Q2785|lt=August 2|1=August 2}}
| prefecture={{ill|Kawasaki-ku|qid=Q1204949|1=Kawasaki-ku, Kawasaki}}
 }}

'''Inage Shrine''' (稲毛神社, Inage Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in [[Kawasaki Ward]], [[:en:Kawasaki City|Kawasaki City]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. Its former Rank under the modern shrine Ranking system was [[Gōsha]] and it is currently a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The shrine was formerly known as '''Sannō Gongen''' and '''Takemikazuchi Shrine''', and it is still commonly referred to as '''Sannō-sama'''.

== Deities ==
The main deity enshrined is [[Takemikazuchi|Takemikazuchi-no-Kami]], with additional deities [[Futsunushi|Futsunushi no Kami]], [[:en:Kikurihime no Kami|Kikurihime no Kami]], [[Izanagi|Izanagi no Mikoto]], and {{ill|Izanami|en|Izanami|qid=Q682306|lt=Izanami no Mikoto|1=Izanami}}.

Until the Edo period, the shrine was said to enshrine {{ill|Oyamakui no Kami|en|Oyamakui no Kami|qid=Q10935105|lt=Sannō Gongen|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}.

== History ==
The exact date of the shrine's founding is unknown, but according to tradition, it began with the enshrinement of Takemikazuchi-no-Kami to pray for the military success of the emperor's army. It is said that Emperor Keikō avoided calamity at the shrine during his eastern expedition. Emperor Kinmei is said to have enshrined the four additional deities during his eastern campaign, and the shrine was subsequently used as a prayer site by successive emperors{{sfn|Kawasaki History Walk}}.

Originally called "Takemikazuchi-no-Miya" after the main deity, the shrine became known as "Kawasaki Sannō Shrine" and "Horinouchi Sannō Gongen" after Kōsaki Motoie enshrined Sannō Gongen in the late Heian period{{sfn|Kawasaki History Walk}}.

In the first year of the Meiji period, Prince Arisugawa Taruhito, on his way to the Kanto region, rested at the shrine and remarked that the name "Sannō" was not suitable under the new government's policy of separating Shinto and Buddhism. The shrine was subsequently renamed "Kawasaki Daijin Inage Shrine" after the old place name of Musashi Province, and the name "Inage Shrine" became established in the mid-Meiji period{{sfn|Kawasaki History Walk}}.

The shrine was designated as a Gōsha in the 6th year of Meiji, and in 1966, it was listed as a Beppyo Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines{{sfn|Kawasaki History Walk}}.

The handwashing stone donated by the family of [[Tanaka Kyugu]] is designated as an important historical monument by Kawasaki City{{sfn|Kawasaki History Walk}}.

== festivals ==
The festival on August 2 is known as the "Kawasaki Sannō festival". On the afternoon of August 2, the "Old-style Miyaza ceremony" (a Kanagawa Prefecture intangible cultural asset) is performed to enshrine the deity in the portable shrine, and the shrine's portable shrine procession by parishioners takes place from the early morning of August 3.

== Managed Shrines ==
* [[Shinden Shrine (Kawasaki City)|Shinden Shrine]] (14-8 Watarida)
* Hachiman Shrine (3-4-8 Ōshima)　　　
* Hachiman Shrine (2-15-1 Nakajima)
